claimStatus: ILLUSTRATIVE, NOT VALIDATED. A single run showed ΔQ=+0.33 (CI excludes 0, 'diversity helps'), but it did NOT replicate: across 3 non-deterministic generation runs ΔQ = [+0.333, +0.111, +0.000] (mean +0.148, sign-unstable, two of three NULL). At N=9 this is consistent with NULL/weak-at-best — the apparent lift was run-to-run noise. A HEADLINE claim requires the _is_validated gate (>=2 judge families, kappa>=0.40, >=3 runs, CI excludes 0 on a larger pre-registered slice), which is DEFERRED.
headline: NULL/inconclusive at N=9: trait diversity does NOT reliably improve council quality (single-run lift did not replicate).
note: The deterministic score_case judge is reproducible; the council GENERATION (ollama 3B) is not, which is exactly why >=3 runs are required. This run vindicates the >=3-run validation discipline: a single run would have over-claimed.
